LAW AND MOTION 302, IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAY 19, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO BRIEF, IF THEY WISH, WHAT THE COURT BELIEVES ARE THE CONTROLLING AUTHORITIES. THE COURT'S OWN RESEARCH DISCLOSES THAT PETITIONER'S FILING OF HIS COMPLAINT SEEKING DAMAGES BEYOND WHAT WAS AWARDED BY THE ARBITRATORS CONSTITUTED A REJECTION OF THE ARBITRATORS' AWARD PER BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 6204(C), WHICH NOW PRECLUDES THE CONFIRMATION OF THAT AWARD ABSENT AN AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. (GIORGIANNI V. CROWLEY (2011) 197 CAL. APP. 4TH 1462, 1468 (NONBINDING AWARD BECOMES BINDING ONLY IF NEITHER SIDE REJECTED IT)). THE PARTIES MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS LIMITED TO THIS ISSUE NO LATER THAN MAY 12, 2016. THE HEARING ON MAY 5, 2016 ON RESPONDENTS' REQUEST TO ACCEPT AN ADR-104 SUBMISSION AS TIMELY WILL ALSO BE CONTINUED TO MAY 19, 2016. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, HANSON AND MURPHY'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. FIRST, THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN THE PETITION BECAUSE THE COURT ALREADY ENTERED JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE. "THE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT ORDINARILY TERMINATES A TRIAL COURT'S JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THE MERITS OF A CASE, APART FROM RULING ON A NEW TRIAL MOTION, A MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT OR A SIMILAR MOTION." (BALLONA WETLANDS LAND TRUST V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (2011) 201 CAL.APP.4TH 455, 479.) SECOND, ON NOVEMBER 20, 2018, HANSON AND MURPHY APPEALED THIS COURT'S ORDER CONFIRMING THE ARBITRATION AWARD AND THE ENSUING JUDGMENT. PER CCP 916, THIS COURT NO LONGER HAS JURISDICTION TO GRANT THE PETITION TO VACATE. THE PETITION TO VACATE IS A PROCEEDING "EMBRACED THEREIN OR AFFECTED" BY THE ORDER CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARD AND JUDGMENT. (CCP 916(A).) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Defendant Kyle Hanson, Mark Murphy'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1285 And 1286.2
Off Calendar Jan-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Kyle Hanson, Mark Murphy'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1285 And 1286.2.

Appearances:
Sidney Luscutoff, Esq. 415-989-7500 of Luscutoff Lendormy & Associates for Plaintiff.
Marcus Mcelhenney, Esq. 215-888-0772 for Defendant Kyle Hanson.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Defendant informs the Court he accepts the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Hanson and Murphy's petition to vacate arbitration award is DENIED. First, the court lacks jurisdiction to entertain the petition because the court already entered judgment in this case. "The entry of judgment ordinarily terminates a trial court's jurisdiction to rule on the merits of a case, apart from ruling on a new trial motion, a motion to vacate the judgment or a similar motion." (Ballona Wetlands Land Trust v. City of Los Angeles (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 455, 479.) Second, on November 20, 2018, Hanson and Murphy appealed this court's order confirming the arbitration award and the ensuing judgment. Per CCP 916, this court no longer has jurisdiction to grant the petition to vacate. The petition to vacate is a proceeding "embraced therein or affected" by the order confirming arbitration award and judgment. (CCP 916(a).)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SIDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF OF THE LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-415-989-7500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARCUS MCELHENNY, tel. no. 1-215-888-0772 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendants Kyle Hanson and Mark Murphy's petition to compel arbitration is denied as to the first cause of action for breach of contract in the first amended complaint and granted as to the second and third causes of action for common counts and fraud in the first amended complaint. The salient language in the legal services agreements is identical in both agreements and provides for adjudication by the State Bar Act fees dispute resolution process only of a "dispute ... as to attorneys' fees and/or costs charged under this Agreement" and "Any other dispute" is to be resolved by a single JAMS arbitrator. The first cause of action alleges entitlement to fees and costs charged per the legal services agreements and thus must be adjudicated in this court. The second and third causes of action do not allege entitlement to fees or costs charged per the legal services agreements and thus must be adjudicated by a single JAMS arbitrator. Per CCP 1281.2, the court stays the first cause of action for breach of contract pending the completion of the JAMS arbitration on the second and third causes of action or until those two causes of action are dismissed. Requiring the JAMS arbitration to proceed prior to the adjudication of the first cause of action promotes judicial economy, especially since all of Mr. Hanson and Mr. Murphy's cross-claims must also be resolved by JAMS arbitration. Even assuming that the concern is justified, for which no showing has been made, Mr. Lendormy's apparent concern that Mr. Hanson and Mr. Murphy will not initiate or pursue the JAMS arbitration or pay the JAMS fees is of modest or no import since Mr. Lendormy has the incentive to initiate and pursue the JAMS arbitration because that is the only forum where his common counts and fraud claims must be adjudicated. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sidney Luscutoff of Luscotoff, Lendormy & Associates telephone number 415-989-7500 for Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy.
Attorney Marcus McElhenney telephone number 215-888-0772 for Defendants Kyle Hanson and Mark Murphy.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is denied. Mr. Lendormy's filing of his complaint on March 10, 2016 within thirty days service of the arbitration award constituted a rejection of the arbitrators' award per Business and Professions Code 6204(c). The filing of the complaint now precludes the confirmation of that award absent an agreement of the parties. There was no need to file a separate rejection of arbitration since no action was pending at the time. (Compare 6204(b)). By seeking damages in excess of what was awarded to Mr. Lendormy in the arbitrators' award, Mr. Lendormy's complaint was, in legal effect, a rejection of the award and a request for a trial after arbitration. The fact that Mr. Lendormy served and attempted to file a rejection of the award the same day he filed his complaint confirms that Mr. Lendormy himself viewed his complaint as a necessary step in rejecting the award. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR #5041. (302/HK)

Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued on the Court's own motion to May 19, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 to give the parties an opportunity to brief, if they wish, what the court believes are the controlling authorities. The court's own research discloses that petitioner's filing of his complaint seeking damages beyond what was awarded by the arbitrators constituted a rejection of the arbitrators' award per Business and Professions Code 6204(c), which now precludes the confirmation of that award absent an agreement of the parties. (Giorgianni v. Crowley (2011) 197 Cal. App. 4th 1462, 1468 (nonbinding award becomes binding only if neither side rejected it)). The parties may file supplemental briefs limited to this issue no later than May 12, 2016. The hearing on May 5, 2016 on respondents' request to accept an ADR-104 submission as timely will also be continued to May 19, 2016.
